programme.title: Seasoning the Seasons

Robbo (Frank Sinatra), Little John (Dean Martin), Will (Sammy Davis Jr.) and company are charitable gangsters fighting for turf in 1928 Chicago.
programme.title: Robin and the Seven Hoods

programme.desc: Two shipwrecked stowaways (Bing Crosby, Bob Hope) ride a camel and meet a princess (Dorothy Lamour), whom they rescue from a desert chief.
programme.title: Road to Morocco

programme.desc: Mark Twain's boy hero (Eddie Hodges) rafts the Mississippi with slave Jim (Archie Moore) and meets a bogus king (Tony Randall) and duke.
programme.title: The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn

programme.desc: Goofy professor Kelp's potion turns him into Buddy Love, a lounge singer at ease with a coed and a crowd.
programme.title: The Nutty Professor

programme.desc: An avenger's (Richard Harrison) quest in the name of his father leads from Mexico to a duel in Texas.
programme.title: Gunfight at Red Sands

programme.desc: A doctor (John Forsythe) sets an expiration date with a rich patient's (Richard Kiley) wife (Reta Shaw).
programme.title: Murder Once Removed

programme.desc: The mythical Greek hero (Reg Park) rescues a maiden left at the mercy of a beast and goes on to save his son from the sexist queen of lost Atlantis (Fay Spain).
programme.title: Hercules and the Captive Women

programme.desc: A man (Mark Damon) seeks revenge after the murder of a relative who left him money.
programme.title: Johnny Yuma

programme.desc: A folksy New England poet (Gary Cooper) inherits $20 million he doesn't want and tells a New York newswoman (Jean Arthur) why.
programme.title: Mr. Deeds Goes to Town

programme.desc: A hit man (Frank Sinatra) and company plan to shoot the president when he gets off his train in Suddenly, Calif.
programme.title: Suddenly

programme.desc: A notorious captain (Barton MacLane) takes a ship on a mission to blow up the Panama Canal with an atomic bomb.
programme.title: Captain Scarface

programme.desc: Gen. Douglas MacArthur (Gregory Peck) returns to the Philippines, manages postwar Japan and defies President Truman (Ed Flanders).
programme.title: MacArthur

programme.desc: The history of 19th-century Western expansion, as seen through the lives of three generations of a pioneer family.
programme.title: How the West Was Won

programme.desc: Railway police take action when kidnappers choose their busy station as the drop-off point for their ransom money.
programme.title: Union Station

programme.desc: S01 E28 — San Francisco Shortly after the Ponderosa cattle herd arrives in San Francisco at the hands of Hoss, Joe and Ben, two of their trail drivers are shanghaied and destined for Hong Kong.
programme.title: Bonanza

programme.desc: Love blooms between a sympathetic attorney and the comely shoplifter he has taken home for the Christmas holiday.
programme.title: Remember the Night

programme.desc: Dorothy tries to save her dog from a storm and eases on down the road to see the Wiz with Tin Man, Scarecrow and Cowardly Lion.
programme.title: The Wiz

programme.desc: Hopalong Cassidy (William Boyd) and his sidekicks (Russell Hayden, Andy Clyde) save a gold miner's daughter from a gambler.
programme.title: Pirates on Horseback

programme.desc: Shakespeare's king of England (Kenneth Branagh) attacks France and declares his love for the French king's daughter.
programme.title: Henry V

programme.desc: The sons (Humphrey Bogart, William Holden) of a Long Island tycoon become romantic rivals for the chauffeur's daughter (Audrey Hepburn).
programme.title: Sabrina

programme.desc: The Sunflower Seed Story Steve travels to South America to resolve the murder of the head of one of the sparring parties which accuses an American citizen.
programme.title: Dangerous Assignment

programme.desc: S02 E06 — Missing Mike is asked to find a missing woman.
programme.title: Man With a Camera

programme.desc: S01 E18 — Made to Fire Blondie takes a job in order to afford a new washing machine, so she hires a maid to take care of the house while she's away. However, the maid's actions make the Bumsteads suspicious.
programme.title: Blondie

programme.desc: S06 E06 — Lucy Gets Jack Benny's Account Lucy designs a vault in an attempt to get Jack Benny's account.
programme.title: The Lucy Show

programme.desc: S01 E30 — Feet of Clay The Cartwrights befriend a boy whose father has been sent to jail and whose mother has recently died.
programme.title: Bonanza

programme.desc: Mark Twain's boy hero (Eddie Hodges) rafts the Mississippi with slave Jim (Archie Moore) and meets a bogus king (Tony Randall) and duke.
programme.title: The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn

programme.desc: Two sailors (Frank Sinatra, Gene Kelly) on leave in Hollywood help an actress (Kathryn Grayson) get her big break.
programme.title: Anchors Aweigh

programme.desc: An outlaw is left by his cohorts and girlfriend to be captured after he is wounded during a heist. When he is released from prison, he tracks his old crew to a run-down town and plays a game of cat and mouse with them.
programme.title: Gun the Man Down

programme.desc: Goofy professor Kelp's potion turns him into Buddy Love, a lounge singer at ease with a coed and a crowd.
programme.title: The Nutty Professor

programme.desc: The more Budapest co-workers (Margaret Sullavan, James Stewart) fight, the more they fall in love as secret pen pals.
programme.title: The Shop Around the Corner

programme.desc: A lady marries an upstart and turns alcoholic in the 1830s Australia.
programme.title: Under Capricorn

programme.desc: Horrible old movies hosted by Dr. Paul Bearer direct from his home, the Tenement Castle.
programme.title: Dr. Paul Bearer's Creature Feature

programme.desc: S01 E30 — Feet of Clay The Cartwrights befriend a boy whose father has been sent to jail and whose mother has recently died.
programme.title: Bonanza

programme.desc: Batman (Adam West) and Robin (Burt Ward) save Gotham City from the Catwoman, Joker (Cesar Romero), Penguin and Riddler.

programme.desc: President Grant helps a cavalry major (Dale Robertson) condemned for helping Dakota Chief Sitting Bull (J. Carrol Naish).
programme.title: Sitting Bull

programme.desc: S01 E09 — Ghost of a Chance Dave and a Mexican woman free a terrorized town from bandits.
programme.title: The Westerner

programme.desc: Father (Spencer Tracy) hears Daughter (Elizabeth Taylor) is pregnant in this sequel to Father of the Bride.

programme.desc: A widowed lawyer with two bright children defends a black man falsely accused of raping a white woman in the 1930s Alabama.
programme.title: To Kill a Mockingbird

programme.desc: A New Yorker and her four sons experience love and laughter from their opinionated but well-meaning family patriarch.

S01 E04 — Letters From Mom Uri is depressed, refusing to leave his bed; Nurit tries to talk to him, but he refuses to answer the phone; Yoske gives him letters, written by his mother when he was a prisoner of war.
programme.title: Prisoners of War

programme.desc: S01 E05 — Keep Your Soul Everyone attends the funeral of Amiel Ben Horin, but Yael feels all alone; she sees her brother everywhere and tries to convince him to leave her alone and let her go on with her life.
programme.title: Prisoners of War

programme.desc: S01 E06 — The Journal Nimrod appears on national TV and recounts his experiences during his 17 years in captivity. He is determined to find a job, but wife Talia thinks it may be too soon.
programme.title: Prisoners of War

programme.desc: S01 E07 — A Picture From Hell Talia and Nimrod enjoy intimate time in their bedroom after the kids leave the house; Dana's relationship with Dr. Ostrovsky becomes more intense; Hazav prepares for his pre-Army tests.
programme.title: Prisoners of War
